narration: What if I told you a single hormone controls your sodium balance?

narration: Meet aldosterone. This hormone, produced by your adrenal glands, plays a crucial role in regulating sodium levels.
visual_description: Context visual: Meet aldosterone. This hormone, produced by your adrenal glands, plays a crucial role in regulating sodium levels.
ai_image_prompt: Context visual: Meet aldosterone. This hormone, produced by your adrenal glands, plays a crucial role in regulating sodium levels.
ai_video_prompt: Cinematic motion: Context visual: Meet aldosterone. This hormone, produced by your adrenal glands, plays a crucial role in regulating sodium levels.

narration: When your sodium drops too low, aldosterone kicks in, signaling your kidneys to retain sodium and excrete potassium.
visual_description: Escalation visual: When your sodium drops too low, aldosterone kicks in, signaling your kidneys to retain sodium and excrete potassium.
ai_image_prompt: Escalation visual: When your sodium drops too low, aldosterone kicks in, signaling your kidneys to retain sodium and excrete potassium.
ai_video_prompt: Cinematic motion: Escalation visual: When your sodium drops too low, aldosterone kicks in, signaling your kidneys to retain sodium and excrete potassium.

narration: But here’s the twist—many factors can disrupt this balance. Stress, diet, and even certain medications can influence aldosterone production.
